sections.title: // risk assessment
paragraphs: Estimated risk assessment: ~80–85% elevated risk. The vessel [energy-progress] (IMO 9387279) is a 2008-built medium-range product/chemical tanker (~46,600 DWT; 183 m × 32 m; service speed ~14–15 kn) whose risk profile is significantly increased due to identity manipulation indicators, including a change of MMSI and operation under a false Guinea flag, both commonly linked to sanctions-evasion practices used by parts of the Russian shadow tanker fleet.

Although factors such as age (~17 years), reflagging history, and ownership transfers can occur in normal tanker markets, the combination of a fraudulent flag, AIS identity changes, and transit through the busy North Sea–English Channel [track] with multiple subsea [cable-crossings] would likely trigger high-priority monitoring by maritime security and coast-watch authorities.

sections.title: // map & data reference
paragraphs: See [track] and [cable-crossings] on map. Click the track (green line) for voyage summary. Click track points for timestamps. Red markers show cable/pipeline crossings — click for details. See Alerts tab for scored points.

properties.name: NEMO Crossing
properties.desc: NEMO interconnector (UK–Belgium, 1000MW HVDC). Crossed at 6 kts, 0312 UTC. No anomalies.

properties.name: NordLink Approach
properties.desc: NordLink HVDC cable (Norway–Germany, 1400MW). Crossed in F7 winds, 3.5m swell. Routine.

properties.name: Viking Link Transit
properties.desc: Viking Link HVDC (UK–Denmark, 1400MW). Tracked parallel for 14nm. AIS alert triggered. Anchor secured, double bridge watch.

properties.name: Norwegian Trench Entry
properties.desc: NPD pipeline cluster crossing. Depth >700m. Reduced speed. Multiple registered pipeline routes in zone.
